gm.name: Mike Borgonzi
gm.title: General Manager
gm.previous_role: Assistant General Manager, Kansas City Chiefs

career_path.role: Co-Director of Player Personnel (with Brett Veach)

scouting_tree.primary_mentor: Scott Pioli (gave first NFL opportunity), Brett Veach (direct supervisor for 8 years as GM)
scouting_tree.philosophical_lineage: Double-lineage: Belichick tree through Scott Pioli, Ron Wolf/Packers tree through John Dorsey. Borgonzi explicitly acknowledged learning 'Bill Belichick's way of scouting' under Pioli, then 'Ron Wolf philosophy' under Dorsey. This creates a hybrid approach combining Patriots-style discipline, character emphasis, and positional value hierarchy with the Packers-style BPA approach and athletic projection.
scouting_tree.breakpoints: None yet observable as Titans GM. His 2025 draft showed willingness to take the consensus top QB (Cam Ward) at #1 overall — not departing from conventional wisdom. Key question is whether he'll follow Chiefs' model of prioritizing trenches and premium positions or adapt to new circumstances.
scouting_tree.lineage_weight_notes: Only 1 draft as Titans GM makes lineage the primary predictive signal. This weight will decrease to 0.50 after 2026 draft and 0.30 after 2027.

key_influencers.name: Robert Saleh
key_influencers.title: Head Coach
key_influencers.known_biases: Defensive-minded coach from Seattle/Pete Carroll tree. Prioritizes defensive line talent, particularly EDGE rushers who can get upfield in his Wide-9 base alignment. Values versatility in defensive backs who can play multiple coverages. Wants 'violence, speed, and length' on defense.

key_influencers.name: Chad Brinker
key_influencers.title: President of Football Operations
key_influencers.known_biases: Former Packers executive. Oversees salary cap, analytics, research. No longer has final roster say as of January 2026 restructure — that now belongs to Borgonzi. Collaborative but Borgonzi leads football decisions.

key_influencers.name: Dave Ziegler
key_influencers.title: Assistant General Manager
key_influencers.known_biases: Former Raiders GM. Brings additional Belichick-tree perspective. Hired by Borgonzi to support scouting operations.

key_influencers.name: Brian Daboll
key_influencers.title: Offensive Coordinator
key_influencers.known_biases: Former Giants HC, Bills OC. Experienced with developing young QBs (Josh Allen). Will influence offensive draft priorities, particularly at skill positions to support Cam Ward.

key_influencers.name: Gus Bradley
key_influencers.title: Defensive Coordinator
key_influencers.known_biases: Pete Carroll disciple. Runs similar Wide-9 system to Saleh. Values length and speed on defense. Long history with Saleh.

front_office.power_dynamics: Borgonzi has clear final say on 53-man roster and draft decisions as of January 2026 restructure. Owner Amy Adams Strunk reports that both Borgonzi and Brinker now report directly to her, but Borgonzi controls football personnel decisions. Coach Saleh reports to Borgonzi. This is a 'GM-first' structure with collaborative input from coaching staff.
front_office.owner_interference_notes: Amy Adams Strunk has fired 3 GMs in less than 5 years (Robinson 2022, Carthon 2025) and has made significant coaching decisions (firing Vrabel, Callahan). Pattern of impatience. Brinker indicated she won't pass on a 'generational talent.' Owner involvement is notable but Borgonzi appears empowered to run football operations.

draft_philosophy.stated_philosophy: Best player available with emphasis on building through the trenches. 'O-line, D-line — those positional values are there.' Also emphasizes 'athleticism, size, and speed' mantra and 'draft picks are our biggest form of currency.'
draft_philosophy.actual_philosophy: Chiefs tree philosophy: Strong preference for premium positions (QB, OT, EDGE, CB, DT) in early rounds. Willing to take BPA at premium positions but unlikely to draft non-premium positions high. Late-round strategy focuses heavily on offensive line, defensive line, and cornerback depth. Has shown willingness to trade up aggressively for targeted players (Worthy trade-up in 2024). Values 'football character' and preparation in prospects.
draft_philosophy.philosophy_mismatch_notes: Insufficient Titans data to identify mismatch. In 2025 draft, took the consensus #1 QB (Ward) at #1 — aligned with both need and BPA. Chiefs history shows stated BPA philosophy but actual behavior weights positional value heavily.

trade_triggers: Targeted premium-position player falling closer than expected (Worthy scenario)
Opportunity to accumulate top-100 picks — stated goal of '12 top-100 picks in 3 years'
Clear tier break on board where value exceeds pick position
trade_behavior.draft_capital_philosophy: Values pick accumulation, particularly in top 100. Stated 'draft picks are our biggest form of currency.' Chiefs history shows willingness to trade both up (for targeted players) and down (for pick quantity). Likely to be aggressive acquiring Day 2 picks.
trade_behavior.trade_history_notes: Chiefs under Veach/Borgonzi frequently traded in drafts — moved up for McDuffie, Worthy, Suamataia. As Titans GM, made T'Vondre Sweat for Jermaine Johnson trade in February 2026 — shows willingness to make bold moves to reshape roster.

position_avoidance: RB in round 1 — Chiefs under Veach's only 1st-round RB (Edwards-Helaire) was seen as a miss. Borgonzi has said RB value can be found late. Titans' reported interest in Jeremiyah Love at #4 would represent a departure if it happens.
position_premium: EDGE — Saleh's defensive philosophy demands premium edge talent. Defensive line investment expected to be high.
OT — Veach/Borgonzi consistently prioritized tackle play for Patrick Mahomes. Same expected for Cam Ward protection.
CB — Chiefs drafted secondary players in 4th round consistently (5 of 7 fourth-round picks). Day 2-3 CB investment expected.

EDGE.archetype_summary: Speed-to-power converter with length who can get upfield in Wide-9 alignment. Saleh/Bradley system wants edge rushers who attack, not two-gappers. Chiefs tree valued George Karlaftis-type: not elite bend but power, motor, and shows up in big moments. Looking for 'violence and length.' Rueben Bain, David Bailey, Arvell Reese all impressed in interviews — all different profiles but share explosive first step.

physical_profile.arm_length_minimum: 32 inches preferred — Bain's shorter arms (31.5") being discussed as potential concern
physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: First step explosion paramount. 40 time matters less than short-area quickness and get-off. Vertical jump as explosion indicator.

playing_style.primary_win_condition: Speed-to-power with ability to convert rush to bull rush. System allows EDGE to 'get up the field and attack' — one-gap penetrators preferred.
least_important_traits: Two-gap ability — not needed in Wide-9
deal_breakers: Pure finesse with no power element
playing_style.traits_that_make_them_reach: Elite production (14+ sacks like Bailey) combined with athletic testing. Late-bloomer trajectory valued (Bailey didn't play football until senior year of high school).

college_production.production_floor: High production valued — Bailey's 14.5 sacks, Bain's CFP dominance. Not a specific number but want 'undeniable' production.
college_production.production_trajectory: Late-bloomer trajectory actually appealing — shows developmental upside
college_production.context_sensitivity: Adjusts for defensive scheme and usage

examples: 2025 2.35 — Femi Oladejo: Raw pass rusher, premium athlete, developmental project. Showed willingness to bet on traits over polish.
Chiefs precedent: George Karlaftis 1.30 (2022) — Power-based, high motor, playoff performer. Felix Anudike-Uzomah 1.31 (2023) — Undersized but explosive.

EDGE.confidence_note: Only 1 EDGE drafted as Titans GM. Saleh's scheme requirements and combine comments about Bain/Bailey/Reese provide direction. Chiefs history adds context.

CB.archetype_summary: Versatile coverage corner who can play man and zone. Saleh's system uses Cover 3, Cover 4/Quarters, and man concepts. Not strictly a press-man requirement but needs physicality. Chiefs under Veach/Borgonzi found late-round CB gems (Sneed R4, Williams R4, Watson R4) — expect Day 2-3 CB investment. Looking for 'identity players who can survive on an island.'

physical_profile.arm_length_minimum: 30 inches — not strict requirement based on Chiefs history
physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Speed important but not eliminator. Trent McDuffie (4.44) was top pick despite not being elite speedster. Processing and football IQ valued highly.

playing_style.primary_coverage_style: Multiple — must be comfortable in both zone (Cover 3/4) and man concepts
least_important_traits: Pure straight-line speed — can compensate with technique
playing_style.traits_that_make_them_reach: Slot versatility — McDuffie draft shows premium placed on nickel-capable corners

college_production.production_floor: No strict INT threshold — McDuffie had modest ball production but elite coverage
college_production.context_sensitivity: High — conference strength and receiving corps faced matters

examples: 2025 6.182 — Marcus Harris: Best tackling CB in class, special teams value. Shows Day 3 CB investment pattern.
Chiefs precedent: L'Jarius Sneed (4.138, 2020) — Versatile, moved from safety to slot to outside. Trent McDuffie (1.21, 2022) — Best nickel in NFL, smooth technique.

CB.confidence_note: Only 1 CB drafted as Titans GM. Combine comments mentioned 'value at top and middle rounds with corner group.' Desperate need at CB going into 2026.

QB.archetype_summary: Playmaking talent with leadership and football character. Borgonzi coming from KC understands 'getting quarterback right' as paramount — watched Chiefs build around Mahomes. Took Cam Ward #1 overall — arm talent, off-script ability, 'alpha personality, natural leadership skills, rare work ethic.' Not looking for game manager; wants QB who can create.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Arm velocity and off-platform ability paramount. Not searching for running QBs specifically but mobility to extend plays valued.

playing_style.primary_style: Off-script playmaker with arm talent. Ward described as 'off-script magician who generates explosive passing plays via creative arm angles and off-platform ability.'
least_important_traits: System-specific fit — Daboll hired to adapt to QB
playing_style.traits_that_make_them_reach: Leadership intangibles — Ward impressed with 'intangible aspects' including work ethic and teammate aura

college_production.production_trajectory: Ward's improvement from 2024 decision to stay in school to 2025 'blossoming' at Miami was valued — development arc matters
college_production.context_sensitivity: High — Ward's Miami success despite challenging circumstances noted

examples: 2025 1.1 — Cam Ward: 6-2, arm talent, leadership, work ethic. Selected despite draft class being 'light on franchise-caliber quarterbacks.' Resisted 'significant trade overtures.'

QB.confidence_note: 1 QB drafted — clear profile established. Unlikely to draft another QB high with Ward as franchise QB.

OT.archetype_summary: Athletic pass protector who can handle speed rush. Chiefs tree consistently prioritized tackle play — Orlando Brown Jr, Kingsley Suamataia investments show profile. Must protect young franchise QB (Ward). Athletic profile important; Chiefs traded up for Suamataia despite concerns about NFL readiness.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Feet/movement skills valued. Suamataia ran 5.04 40 at 326 pounds. Looking for athletes.

playing_style.primary_style: Pass protection first with zone-blocking capability
least_important_traits: Pure mauler run-blocking — zone scheme doesn't require
playing_style.traits_that_make_them_reach: Athletic profile that projects even if not polished

college_production.production_trajectory: Development from RT to LT shows progression (Suamataia path)

examples: No OT drafted in 2025 Titans draft.
Chiefs precedent: Kingsley Suamataia (2.63, 2024) — Athletic, traded up for despite rawness. Orlando Brown Jr trade acquisition shows tackle value.

OT.confidence_note: No OT drafted as Titans GM. Chiefs history and Cam Ward protection priority inform projections.

IOL.archetype_summary: Intelligent, athletic interior linemen who can pass protect and pull. Chiefs 2021 draft revolutionized their OL with Creed Humphrey (C) and Trey Smith (G) — both became Pro Bowlers. Premium on processing, strength, and movement skills. Can take center or guard; versatility valued.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Short shuttle and 3-cone for movement skills. Strength testing matters.

playing_style.primary_style: Pass protection with ability to pull and reach-block in zone scheme
least_important_traits: Elite power run blocking — scheme doesn't demand pure maulers

college_production.context_sensitivity: Competition level and scheme fit matter

examples: 2025 4.136 — Jackson Slater: FCS (Sacramento State), dominated Senior Bowl, G/C versatility. Shows willingness to take small-school talent if they prove themselves against competition.
Chiefs precedent: Creed Humphrey (2.63, 2021) — Best center in NFL. Trey Smith (6.226, 2021) — Late-round gem, now Pro Bowler.

IOL.confidence_note: 1 IOL drafted. Chiefs IOL success provides strong template.

DT.archetype_summary: Mix of run-stuffing nose and penetrating 3-tech. Titans have Jeffery Simmons (elite) and T'Vondre Sweat (nose tackle). Need complements, not replacements. Chiefs under Veach drafted Chris Jones-complement types — rotational pieces who can generate interior pressure.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: First step quickness for 3-tech. Strength testing for nose.

least_important_traits: Pure two-gap ability — system is one-gap

examples: No DT drafted in 2025 Titans draft.
Traded for T'Vondre Sweat (gave up Jermaine Johnson) — shows DT value. Sweat is nose tackle, not 3-tech.
Chiefs precedent: Day 3 DT investments primarily.

DT.confidence_note: No DT drafted. Simmons/Sweat presence reduces immediate need but 3-tech depth needed.

WR.archetype_summary: Playmaking receivers who can create after the catch and stretch the field. Chiefs under Veach used Day 2 for WRs (Rice, Moore, Hardman) and traded up for Worthy (elite speed). Borgonzi emphasized 'receiver group is a good group' in this draft. Titans desperately need WR1 for Cam Ward. Looking for 'explosive' playmakers.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Speed valued — Worthy (4.21 40), Hardman (4.33). YAC ability important.

playing_style.primary_style: Explosive playmakers who create YAC opportunities
most_valued_traits: Contested catch ability for X receivers
least_important_traits: Blocking — not a priority for WR evaluation

college_production.production_floor: High production expected — Chiefs WRs had college production
college_production.context_sensitivity: Receiving room competition and QB quality adjusted

examples: 2025 4.101 — Chimere Dike: Speed, downfield ability, ball-tracking. Day 2 speed receiver.
2025 4.136 — Elic Ayomanor: Big-bodied, physical, contested catch winner. X receiver profile. Trade up for him.
2025 4.142 — Gunnar Helm (TE, but receiving profile): Pass-catching tight end investment shows receiving priority.
Chiefs precedent: Xavier Worthy (1.28, 2024) — Elite speed. Rashee Rice (2.55, 2023) — Route runner, YAC. Skyy Moore (2.54, 2022).

WR.confidence_note: 3 pass catchers drafted in 2025 on Day 2-3. Pattern of accumulating receiving options evident.

TE.archetype_summary: Receiving tight ends who can stretch the seam. Not blocking-first TEs. Gunnar Helm draft shows profile — pass-catching ability over blocking. Chiefs (Kelce influence) value TEs who create mismatches.

playing_style.primary_style: Receiving TE who can flex and create mismatches
least_important_traits: Inline blocking — not primary evaluation criterion

examples: 2025 4.142 — Gunnar Helm: 'Soft-handed' receiving TE. Pass-catching focus.
Chiefs precedent: Travis Kelce (team, not drafted by Veach) influence. Jared Wiley (4.131, 2024) — receiving upside.

S.archetype_summary: Versatile safeties who can play multiple roles. Saleh's system uses 'vision safety' concepts in Quarters coverage — need safeties who can rotate, play single-high, and come down into the box. Chiefs found Bryan Cook (hybrid) and others who can wear multiple hats.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Range and closing speed. Coverage ability paramount.

playing_style.primary_style: Hybrid safety who can play single-high, split-field, and drop into box
least_important_traits: Pure box safety profile — need coverage ability
playing_style.traits_that_make_them_reach: True centerfield range with box ability

examples: 2025 3.66 — Kevin Winston Jr.: Hard hitter, zone coverage, would be top-50 if healthy. Injury risk taken for talent.
Chiefs precedent: Bryan Cook (2.62, 2022) — Versatile, became defensive centerpiece. Jaden Hicks (4.132, 2024).

S.confidence_note: 1 S drafted. Winston selection shows willingness to bet on talent despite injury concern.

LB.archetype_summary: Coverage linebackers who can process quickly. Saleh's scheme calls LBs 'down safeties' — they need to cover backs and TEs while reading routes. Not looking for pure thumpers. Nick Bolton (Chiefs 2.58, 2021) is template — tackles, coverage, instincts.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Speed and lateral agility. Coverage testing matters.

playing_style.primary_style: Coverage-capable linebacker who can run sideline to sideline
playing_style.traits_that_make_them_reach: Elite coverage skills — Fred Warner type

examples: No LB drafted in 2025 Titans draft.
Combine: Borgonzi said 'good linebacker group' in draft. Arvell Reese interviewed well — could play EDGE or LB.
Chiefs precedent: Nick Bolton (2.58, 2021) — 458 tackles in 4 seasons, coverage ability. Leo Chenal (3.103, 2022) — Blitzer.

LB.confidence_note: No LB drafted. Saleh system requirements and Chiefs history inform projections.

RB.archetype_summary: Pass-catching backs who can contribute on all downs. Chiefs found Isiah Pacheco in 7th round — shows RB value can be found late. Veach explicitly said RBs with 1,000-yard seasons go later than toolsy linemen. Not a premium position for early investment. However, Titans reportedly interested in Jeremiyah Love at #4 — would be significant departure.

physical_profile.key_athletic_tests: Speed and burst. Pass-catching ability tested.

playing_style.traits_that_make_them_reach: Elite explosiveness with receiving ability — Love's 4.37 40 and 18 TDs

college_production.production_floor: Production valued but RBs go later with same production as OL/DL with potential

examples: 2025 6.202 — Kalel Mullings: Michigan, 948 yards, 12 TDs. Day 3 investment shows RB value philosophy.
Chiefs precedent: Isiah Pacheco (7.251, 2022) — Playoff hero, 7th round find. Clyde Edwards-Helaire (1.32, 2020) — Considered miss at that value.

RB.confidence_note: 1 RB drafted in late rounds. Love interest would represent departure from Chiefs philosophy.

reach_tolerance.notes: Chiefs/Borgonzi philosophy: willing to trade up for targeted players (Worthy, Suamataia) but generally stick close to board. Femi Oladejo in 2025 was seen as slight reach for rawness. Will reach moderately for scheme fits and premium athletes.

scheme_fit.offensive_scheme: Brian Daboll offense. Pro-style concepts adapted to QB. Zone-run elements with play-action. Will build around Cam Ward's improvisational ability. Josh Allen development template suggests patience with raw tools.
scheme_fit.defensive_scheme: Robert Saleh Wide-9 base, 4-3 front. Mix of Cover 3 and Cover 4/Quarters. One-gap penetration principles. Values speed and length on edge, versatile defensive backs, coverage linebackers. Saleh called it 'fast and violent' with emphasis on creating chaos up front.
scheme_fit.scheme_change_notes: Major scheme change from Brian Callahan to Robert Saleh in January 2026. Defensive personnel requirements shifted significantly. Previous Callahan system was more varied; Saleh brings defined Wide-9 philosophy that affects draft priorities at EDGE, LB, CB, S.

psychological_profile.risk_description: Moderate risk tolerance. Winston (injury) and Oladejo (rawness) picks show willingness to bet on upside. But generally takes high-floor players at premium positions. Chiefs tree historically conservative in round 1, more aggressive on Day 2-3.
psychological_profile.recency_description: Lower recency bias than average. Chiefs' sustained success came from consistent philosophy, not reactive picks. However, Titans' desperate state may force more need-driven picks than Borgonzi prefers.
psychological_profile.sunk_cost_behavior: Will move on from mistakes quickly. Chiefs cut/traded players when they didn't work. As Titans GM, inherited Will Levis but drafted Ward #1 — didn't let prior regime's investment dictate decision.
psychological_profile.combine_description: Moderate combine weight. Stated 'athleticism, size, and speed mantra' at combine. Chiefs history shows athletic testing matters but film-first at premium positions. Will reach for elite athletes (Oladejo, Worthy) but not at expense of production.
psychological_profile.character_description: High character standards from Pioli/Belichick tree. 'Football character' emphasized — how players prepare, work, 'treat their bodies right.' Ward selection cited 'alpha personality, natural leadership skills, rare work ethic.' Character is a differentiator.
psychological_profile.quarterback_philosophy: Build around franchise QB. Chiefs model: get QB right (Mahomes), then protect him and give him weapons. Ward selection at #1 shows commitment. Will invest heavily in protecting and supporting Ward.

college_production_preferences.production_vs_projection: Balanced. Values production but will bet on traits when athletic profile is exceptional (Oladejo). Veach quote: 'Running backs with 1,000 yards for three years go later than linemen who have potential.' Production matters more at premium positions than developmental ones.

production_thresholds_by_position.WR: High production expected — targeted WRs with meaningful college production
production_thresholds_by_position.EDGE: Production valued but will project on elite athletes
production_thresholds_by_position.CB: Ball production less important than coverage film

college_production_preferences.notes: Ward's improvement from '24 to '25 (late bloomer) was valued. Bailey's trajectory (didn't play until senior year HS) appealing. Not strictly early-producer focused.

CB.notes: McDuffie not elite speedster but still top pick. Football IQ can compensate. Tackling matters.

EDGE.notes: Get-off and explosion valued over 40 time. Length preferred but Bain's 31.5" arms being evaluated.

WR.notes: Worthy (4.21), Dike (speed emphasis). Speed-based playmakers valued.

OT.notes: Suamataia 5.04 40 at 326 lbs. Looking for athletes who can pass protect.

RB.notes: Love's 4.37 gets attention. Pacheco found late despite speed.

measurable_thresholds.threshold_type: Soft preferences rather than hard cutoffs. Will take players who break thresholds if film is exceptional.

size_preferences_by_position.CB: 5-10 to 6-2, varied — slot versatility adds value

measurable_thresholds.athletic_testing_favorites: First step explosion tests for EDGE, short shuttle/3-cone for CB and LB, vertical jump as explosion proxy, 40 time for WR and RB
measurable_thresholds.measurables_vs_film: Film-first with measurables as confirming/eliminating factors. Won't override great film for bad testing unless testing reveals scheme-specific limitation.

round_strategy.round_1_strategy: Premium positions only — QB, EDGE, OT, CB, possibly elite DT or WR. Take best available at premium position. Willing to trade up if targeted player is obtainable. Will not reach dramatically but will pay small premium for guy they love.
round_strategy.round_2_3_strategy: Fill needs at premium positions. CB, WR, EDGE depth. This is where production expectations are highest — look for contributors. Chiefs found Bolton, Cook, Chenal here.
round_strategy.day_3_strategy: Developmental athletes and scheme fits. Heavy IOL and DB investment pattern from Chiefs. Look for 'hidden talent' where team values differ from consensus. Special teams contributors. Willing to take fliers on traits over production here.
round_strategy.compensatory_pick_usage: Use comp picks for slightly higher-risk prospects. Titans may acquire comp picks via trade-downs.

hit_rate_by_position.note: Insufficient Titans data. Chiefs history during Borgonzi tenure (2009-2024) as reference:

QB.note: No QB drafted with Veach/Borgonzi — Mahomes predates

EDGE.note: Karlaftis, Anudike solid contributors. Chiefs EDGE hit rate decent.

IOL.note: Humphrey, Smith elite. Strong IOL evaluation.

CB.note: McDuffie, Sneed, Watson, Williams. Elite CB evaluation, especially Day 2-3.

WR.note: Rice suspended, Moore inconsistent, Hardman limited, Worthy promising. WR mixed.

blind_spots: WR evaluation at premium investment level — Rice and Moore didn't become alpha receivers. Day 2 WRs mixed results.

free_agency_draft_interaction.fa_spending_philosophy: Moderate to aggressive in FA to fill immediate needs, then draft BPA at remaining needs. Chiefs model: signed key FAs to fill holes, drafted depth/development. Titans have $93M+ cap space — expect significant FA spending before draft.
free_agency_draft_interaction.fa_draft_correlation: When FA signing fills need, that position drops in draft priority but still may be addressed for depth. Jermaine Johnson trade filled EDGE need but EDGE still possible at #4.
free_agency_draft_interaction.roster_construction_philosophy: Draft, develop, retain core players. Build through draft at premium positions, supplement via FA. Stated goal: '12 top-100 picks in 3 years' — values draft capital accumulation.

notable_patterns: 2025 draft: QB at #1 (Ward), EDGE at #35 (Oladejo), S at #66 (Winston), then 6 Day 3 picks on offense
Heavy Day 2-3 investment in pass catchers (Dike, Ayomanor, Helm)
Showed willingness to bet on upside over polish (Oladejo raw, Winston injury, Slater small-school)
No OT or CB drafted in 2025 despite need — addressed in FA
Pattern suggests: premium positions in rounds 1-2, depth/development on Day 3

biggest_reaches.context: Raw but premium athlete. Scheme fit for Saleh. Criticized as reach but fits 'athleticism, size, speed' mantra.

biggest_steals.context: Would've been top-50 without injury. Borgonzi willing to take medical risk for talent.

confidence_notes: Only 1 draft as Titans GM (2025). Profile heavily relies on Chiefs lineage (16 years of experience) and stated philosophy from combine interviews. Saleh's scheme requirements add significant new variable. Profile should be substantially updated after 2026 draft when Borgonzi has a second draft with his own coaching staff and clearer vision. Key watch items: Will he deviate from Chiefs' RB-late philosophy for Love? How aggressive will he be trading for picks?
